{{Quote|Young master, have you heard the tale of the Jedi Master Murrtaggh and the Dark Underlord?|A Bimm bard|<ref name="HR">''[[Heroes & Rogues]]''</ref>}}
'''Murrtaggh''' was a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] active during the [[New Sith Wars]]<ref name="Evil" /> of [[2000 BBY/Legends|2000 BBY]]–[[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> At one point he negotiated a deal with [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] of the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] warrior culture, hiring them to stage a diversionary attack on<ref name="Evil" /> the camp of<ref name="Insider80">{{InsiderCite|80|The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors}}</ref> the [[Dark Underlord]], a mysterious and powerful leader of the [[New Sith]]—the then-current incarnation of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]], the ideological antithesis of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. During the ensuing<ref name="Evil" /> [[1750 BBY]] [[Battle of Malrev IV|battle]] on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]<ref name="TEA" /> [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="CSWE Malrev">{{CSWECite|II|282|Malrev IV}}</ref> [[Malrev IV]], Murrtaggh sneaked into the Dark Underlord's territory and [[Assassin/Legends|assassinated]] the Sith, although in the process he himself fell to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]].<ref name="Evil" /> By the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]<ref name="HR" /> more than a [[Millennium/Legends|millennium]] later,<ref name="TEA" /> the history of Murrtaggh and the Dark Underlord had become immortalized in a tale told by a [[Bimm/Legends|Bimm]] [[Unidentified bard|bard]] whose repertoire of stories and [[Music/Legends|songs]] focused on the exploits of the Jedi.<ref name="HR" />

==Behind the scenes==
Murrtaggh was first mentioned in the [[1995]] sourcebook ''[[Heroes & Rogues]]'', authored by [[Rick D. Stuart|Rick Stuart]] and [[Paul Sudlow]] and published for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="HR" />
